opp.name: Bluescope Steel_S2P_2026
opp.next_step: Confidence:25% 30 JUN - Luke has reached out to Prue Jacobson 12th June - Need to requalify as contact has been very quiet. 1st June: Need to requalify timeline, as many organizational changes have been made internally. March 2026 Prue Jacobson (Head of Global Supplier Relationship Management (Group Technology)) is interested in knowing more about Zycus. Agreed to come for a meeting this month but had to back out due to unavailability of time slot. Will attend Horizon 2026.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 258 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single Mar 2026 entry ‚Äî the full content reads: 'Prue Jacobson (Head of Global Supplier Relationship Management (Group Technology)) is interested in knowing more about Zycus. Agreed to come for a meeting this month but had to back out due to unavailability of time slot. Will attend Horizon 2026.' Zero AI mention despite Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA all in product scope.
• Prue Jacobson is named as the buyer-side stakeholder (Head of Global Supplier Relationship Management within Group Technology). The captured engagement is essentially a missed-meeting placeholder with a Horizon 2026 deferral. No demo, no use cases, no AI champion language.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is thin (258 chars, one note). Bluescope has a strong AI product loadout (Merlin + Agentic AI + ANA) but the rep-buyer dialogue is essentially non-existent beyond a single Horizon deferral. Score 5 (boundary DOWN) reflects AI scope without AI conversation ‚Äî would re-evaluate post-Horizon engagement.

champion.narrative: Champion weak and cooling. Prue Jacobson (Head of Global Supplier Relationship Management, Group Technology) is the sole buyer-side contact. Next_Step__c March 2026 (~4 months ago) stated she 'is interested in knowing more about Zycus', agreed to a meeting but backed out due to unavailability, and committed to attend Horizon 2026. Two scheduled meetings were cancelled (2025-09-23, 2026-03-17). By 2026-06-12 the rep noted 'need to requalify as contact has been very quiet'. On 2026-06-30 (today) the rep reached out again awaiting response. Confidence is 25%. There is no evidence of advocacy (no introductions to Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or procurement leadership; no internal champion actions visible in calls or tasks). Access to power is unknown (no EB/DM mapped). Current engagement is passive and cooling (unresponsive for weeks, requalification overdue). The ranked move is to re-engage the contact with a value-anchor (e.g. Horizon 2026 invitation, a relevant BlueScope-peer case study, or a specific AI procurement use case), assess whether she remains a viable champion, and if she is not the right thread, ask her to introduce the procurement transformation or S2P technology sponsor who owns the buying decision.

north_star_verdict.headline: Single-thread deal with cooling buyer engagement and 131 days in Qualified with no progression. Buyer interest expressed in March 2026 (~4 months ago), cancelled two scheduled meetings (September 2025 and March 2026), then went quiet. Rep noted requalification needed on 2026-06-01 and 2026-06-12 due to buyer silence and organizational changes. Rep outreach on 2026-06-30 (today) awaits response. Close date 2026-12-01 (154 days out) will slip without immediate buyer re-engagement and multi-threading beyond the single cooling contact.

flags: Champion at risk of complete fade. Prue's early advocacy (Feb 2026 'go for Zycus instead' signal) has not translated to engagement: 2 cancelled calls, no Horizon confirmation, 2 unanswered May emails, and Next Step now rates her 25% confidence. Either rebuild the Prue relationship (direct call, not email, to understand the organizational change impact and requalify her role and authority) or pivot to a new champion who can drive the deal post-reorganization.

Zero discovery means zero validated fit. No transcribed buyer conversations, no documented requirements, no pain quantification, no use-case validation, and no demo have occurred despite 102 days in Qualified stage. The Feb 2026 relationship signal is not a substitute for discovery. Before any proposal or pricing, conduct a full discovery session with a multi-stakeholder group (Procurement, Technology, Finance) to surface the business problem, quantify the pain, map decision criteria, validate the S2P + Merlin/AI scope, and anchor the solution to BlueScope's operational reality and post-reorganization priorities.
Organizational changes disclosed 2026-06-01 have not been requalified for impact on timeline, budget authority, or stakeholder roles
MEDDPICC completely undocumented: no metrics, no quantified pain, no decision criteria, no process, no competition mapped
Horizon 2026 attendance commitment from March 2026 remains unconfirmed; no logistics or follow-up locked in
best_practice_check.summary: Three critical win-strategy levers for this deal: (1) Re-engage and requalify immediately to assess whether the opportunity is still live post-organizational-changes and whether the cooling single-thread contact remains viable or should hand off to the right sponsor. (2) Multi-thread aggressively beyond the single Evaluator to map the Economic Buyer (CFO, CPO, or procurement transformation sponsor) and build redundancy, given the extreme single-point-of-failure risk in a $300k S2P platform deal at a large manufacturer. (3) Run discovery that elicits the specific procurement pain and business case (spend visibility, supplier fragmentation, AI automation opportunity) and positions the Merlin agentic AI story as the wedge, given the full S2P suite scope (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) is in play but the buyer's prioritization and fit criteria are entirely unmapped.

customer_preference.evidence: Zero completed buyer calls; two scheduled meetings cancelled (2025-09-23, 2026-03-17); Next_Step__c March 2026 states buyer 'interested in knowing more about Zycus' but no preference expressed, no positive comparison vs competitors, no Zycus-specific next steps requested beyond cancelled meeting; contact unresponsive for weeks by mid-June 2026

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. No competitor, incumbent vendor, or current-state solution has been documented in any Salesforce field or call note. The deal cannot be positioned without understanding what BlueScope is using today and who else they are evaluating.

Products__c includes Merlin and Agentic AI, signaling intent to position the AI-driven sourcing and negotiation story, but with zero completed discovery calls the buyer's AI appetite, current AI maturity, and receptiveness to the Merlin narrative are entirely unmapped. No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated. No call evidence of AI use-case discussion, ROI workshop, or Merlin demo. With 131 days in Qualified and no discovery, AI positioning has not been attempted. The ranked move is to run a discovery call that assesses the buyer's AI procurement needs (sourcing automation, contract intelligence, negotiation agent, supplier-risk monitoring) and positions Merlin as the wedge against the incumbent or competitive tooling.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations unknown. Zero completed discovery or demo calls, no documented business requirements, evaluation criteria, or fit discussion in any field or activity. Products__c lists a full S2P suite (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) but the buyer's prioritization (e.g. contract lifecycle vs AI-driven sourcing vs invoice automation), must-have features, integration requirements (ERP system), user volume, and fit scorecard are undocumented. With 131 days in Qualified and no discovery, customer expectations are entirely unestablished. The ranked move is to run a discovery call that maps the buyer's current procurement challenges, top priorities, and evaluation criteria, then position Zycus against those needs.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 258 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single Mar 2026 entry ‚Äî the full content reads: 'Prue Jacobson (Head of Global Supplier Relationship Management (Group Technology)) is interested in knowing more about Zycus. Agreed to come for a meeting this month but had to back out due to unavailability of time slot. Will attend Horizon 2026.' Zero AI mention despite Merlin, Agentic AI and ANA all in product scope.
• Prue Jacobson is named as the buyer-side stakeholder (Head of Global Supplier Relationship Management within Group Technology). The captured engagement is essentially a missed-meeting placeholder with a Horizon 2026 deferral. No demo, no use cases, no AI champion language.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is thin (258 chars, one note). Bluescope has a strong AI product loadout (Merlin + Agentic AI + ANA) but the rep-buyer dialogue is essentially non-existent beyond a single Horizon deferral. Score 5 (boundary DOWN) reflects AI scope without AI conversation ‚Äî would re-evaluate post-Horizon engagement.

102 days in Qualified (far above norm), zero forward progress, 25% rep confidence, requalification urgently overdue, close date 2026-12-01 (158 days out) likely slips if buyer engagement does not resume immediately

Single-thread to one cooling contact (Prue Jacobson, 37 days unresponsive, 25% rep confidence) in a $300k enterprise deal at a 16,500-person public manufacturer is extreme risk. Multi-thread immediately: reach Procurement Manager (Lee Garfield, Jason McCallum, Sue Casimir), CTO (Jagdeep Singh), or Finance (Kate Wadwell) to build redundancy and map the real buying committee.

Champion at risk of complete fade. Prue's early advocacy (Feb 2026 'go for Zycus instead' signal) has not translated to engagement: 2 cancelled calls, no Horizon confirmation, 2 unanswered May emails, and Next Step now rates her 25% confidence. Either rebuild the Prue relationship (direct call, not email, to understand the organizational change impact and requalify her role and authority) or pivot to a new champion who can drive the deal post-reorganization.

Zero discovery means zero validated fit. No transcribed buyer conversations, no documented requirements, no pain quantification, no use-case validation, and no demo have occurred despite 102 days in Qualified stage. The Feb 2026 relationship signal is not a substitute for discovery. Before any proposal or pricing, conduct a full discovery session with a multi-stakeholder group (Procurement, Technology, Finance) to surface the business problem, quantify the pain, map decision criteria, validate the S2P + Merlin/AI scope, and anchor the solution to BlueScope's operational reality and post-reorganization priorities.

value.flag: Multi-thread beyond the single Evaluator to map Economic Buyer and build redundancy. A $300k S2P platform deal at a large public manufacturer (BlueScope Steel, ~$10B revenue) requires C-level or CFO/CPO sponsorship; relying on one cooling mid-level contact with no commercial authority is extreme single-point-of-failure risk. Ask the Evaluator to introduce the procurement transformation or S2P technology budget owner.
